data.narrative:
Raphe mPhibr is a Series B-stage Defense & Security company founded by Vivek and Vikash Mishra that has raised $145M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion ($1.0B), alongside 2 public defense-focused comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, anchoring on the $145M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $276M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 private comparables produces $1.1B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $1.5B. The divergence between methods reflects uncertainty in whether Raphe operates at the lower or upper end of Defense & Security Series B funding profiles.

The triangulated valuation range spans $234M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $700M. This wide dispersion reflects two competing signals: the company's relatively modest capital raise relative to peer funding levels, and peer valuations that cluster above $1.0B. Key uncertainties include the maturity and revenue traction of Raphe versus its comparables, and whether recent Defense & Security funding inflation warrants upward or downward adjustment to peer multiples.
